content.plain_english: This week in Year 4 maths, your child is working on Multiplication and division B. The learning focuses on: add and subtract numbers with up to four digits using the formal written methods of columnar addition and subtraction where appropriate and solve addition and subtraction two-step problems in contexts, deciding which operations and methods to use and why. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: In lessons, children start with concrete resources — base ten blocks, counters, or number lines — to model calculations physically. They then represent what they have done using drawings and diagrams before practising the formal written method independently. This concrete → pictorial → abstract approach helps the method become secure before moving on.
content.home_activity: Quiz each other on the times tables your child is focusing on this week. You say a question, they answer, then swap. Try it as a speed challenge: how many can they answer correctly in one minute? Mix up the order so it does not become just a chant — recall is the goal.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to add and subtract numbers with up to four digits using the formal written methods of columnar addition and subtraction where appropriate?
Was it easy or tricky to solve addition and subtraction two-step problems in contexts, deciding which operations and methods to use and why? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: The result of multiplying two numbers together.
A number that divides exactly into another number.
The result when one number is divided by another.
The number you are dividing by.
A written, columnar way of setting out a calculation.

example_questions: A box holds 24 crayons. How many in 15 boxes?
content.times_table_tip: Year 4 children should know all their times tables to 12 × 12 by the end of the year — the national Multiplication Tables Check happens in Year 4. Daily 3-minute practice mixing tables in random order is the best preparation.
